text:
Name:  Weiss,Douglas               Unit No:   f9739d94-6c30-e202-d2d4-2daa9eb31f8a

Admission Date:  1986-07-18              Discharge Date:   1994-05-31

Date of Birth:  1973-02-25             Sex:   F

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Penicillins / Keflex / Clindamycin / Vancomycin

Attending:Justin
Addendum:
Pt was diagnosed with MRSA

Hospital stay delayed because of bed non availability, Also pt
had nuero consult for myalgia / myopathy. Below is her
consultationnote:

In summary, this is an 88 year old female with a history of
proximal muscle weakness that started several months ago, but it
is unclear the exact time of onset.  She clearly reports being
unable to comb her hair due to shoulder weakness prior to the
onset of her current complex hospitalization.

Specifically, the patient is fully alert and oriented with
normalcognition.  She currently has neck flexors and
extensors,thatalthough slightly weak, may be appropriate for
age.  Her shoulder girdles demonstrate marked wasting with 2/5
strength at thedeltoids, supraspinatus and infraspinatus.  The
patient has 4+/5strength in the biceps, with only partial
activation (suggestinglikely C5 weakness with C6 relatively
spared).  Distal strengthin the upper ext slightly reduced at
4+/5.  Hip flexors 06-07 onthe left, 12-19 on the right. Distal
strength better at 4+/5 butslightly weaker on the right than
left.  Sensory exam revealsdistal decrease to vibration and
proprioception with somevariable sensory changes in the upper
extremities.  Reflexespresent (2's) everywhere except ankles
(absent).  Toes upgoingbilaterally.

Assessment:

This is an 88 year old female with multifactorial
weakness that is more proximal than distal.  The patient clearly
has evidence for myelopathy with upgoing toes and substantial
C-spine disease present in 05-07 by MRI scan (but suboptimal
images).  This is likely making a large contribution to her
strength.  She also has an axonal polyneuropathy and a
generalized myopathy.  Most of her laboratory studies have been
normal, with no evidence for elevated CK.  The patient does have
a mildly elevated CRP and ESR, but not unexpectedly considering
her hospital course.

I would recommend vigorous physical therapy to prevent
contractures, and improve strength from deconditioning.  She
should have another MRI of her C-spine (the last one had too
much motion artifact to actually determine the contribution it
may have on her presentation).  She is currently on a Statin,
you may want to consider an alternative Smith.  Further
evaluation of her myopathy could require a muscle biopsy, would
recommend she follow up with the neuromuscular department as an
outpatient to consider this possibility.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Sarah D Culbertson Memorial Hospital - 143 Marc Views
Brewerstad, IL 44542-9624
